user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can have Hermes session starts report managed status so new Hermes sessions are visible in Janus Code.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes on_session_start, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and the generated plugin posts normalized hook payloads to Janus Code.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can see Hermes model work before an LLM call starts so active prompt processing is visible.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes pre_llm_call, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and tests verify registered plugin hooks post normalized JSON to Janus Code.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can see Hermes model work after an LLM call completes so managed status reflects prompt progress.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes post_llm_call, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and the generated plugin normalizes event payloads before posting.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can see Hermes tool work before a tool call starts so long-running tool activity does not appear idle.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes pre_tool_call, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and generated payload normalization bounds tool inputs before JSON encoding.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can see Hermes tool results after a tool call completes so managed status reflects tool progress.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes post_tool_call, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and tests verify bounded generated plugin payload normalization before JSON encoding.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can see Hermes approval requests before they block so approval waits are visible in managed status.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes pre_approval_request, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and the plugin posts through the shared Hermes hook endpoint.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can see Hermes approval responses so managed status can leave waiting state after a decision.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes post_approval_response, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and the plugin sends normalized payloads through Janus Code's Hermes hook endpoint.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can have Hermes session endings finish managed status so completed Hermes work does not remain active.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes on_session_end, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and plugin enablement is required for installed status.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can have Hermes finalization events close out managed session state after cleanup completes.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes on_session_finalize, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and generated plugin files are status-checked as Orca-managed.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can have Hermes session resets clear stale managed status before a new session continues.
expected_behavior: The Hermes event registry includes on_session_reset, the managed plugin manifest declares it under provides_hooks, and stale or disabled plugin states are reported as partial installs.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open GitLab.com remotes without configuring a custom GitLab host so hosted review and task flows work for standard GitLab projects.
expected_behavior: The GitLab default host registry includes gitlab.com, GitLab project ref parsing accepts HTTPS and SSH GitLab.com remotes by default, and non-default hosts still require explicit known-host discovery.

expected_behavior: The forge provider registry includes GitHub after GitLab in detection order, resolves repositories through GitHub remote context, supports review creation, and adapts GitHub branch lookup through the shared forge provider contract.

expected_behavior: The forge provider registry includes Bitbucket as a read-only review provider, resolves repositories through Bitbucket remote context, and maps Bitbucket pull request data into the shared hosted-review model without enabling review creation.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can open Source Control so staged files, changed files, commits, and hosted review actions are reachable in-app.
expected_behavior: The source-control route renders SourceControl and keeps file actions, commit generation, branch actions, and hosted review operations scoped to the active worktree owner.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can configure and run the Commit message source-control AI action so staged changes get an appropriate commit summary.
expected_behavior: The source-control action registry includes commitMessage as a text action, exposes labels, variables, default templates, action recipes, and generation resolves the configured global or repo-specific agent safely.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can configure and run the Pull request source-control AI action so PR title and body drafts include branch diff context.
expected_behavior: The source-control action registry includes pullRequest as a text action, exposes PR-specific variables and templates, and PR generation resolves the configured source-control AI recipe without assuming a GitHub-only provider path.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can configure and run the Branch name source-control AI action so workspace branch names can be generated from prompt context.
expected_behavior: The source-control action registry includes branchName as a text action, exposes first prompt and assistant message variables, and recipe normalization keeps agent and command-template settings bounded.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can configure and launch the Commit failure fixes action so an agent can address failed commit attempts with the right context.
expected_behavior: The source-control action registry includes fixCommitFailure as a launch action, the launch dialog resolves recipe agent/template settings, and commit-failure launch support picks the configured agent for recovery work.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can configure and launch the Broken checks fixes action so an agent can work from failing check context.
expected_behavior: The source-control action registry includes fixChecks as a launch action, Checks and Source Control surfaces resolve its recipe, and the launch dialog can save global or repo-specific action settings.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can configure and launch the Conflict resolution action so an agent can help resolve merge conflicts with source-control context.
expected_behavior: The source-control action registry includes resolveConflicts as a launch action, Source Control resolves its recipe and launch platform, and action dialog state supports agent delivery and saved command templates.

user_story: As a Janus Code user, I can configure and launch the Review comment resolution action so an agent can address review feedback from source-control context.
expected_behavior: The source-control action registry includes resolveComments as a launch action, Checks and Source Control can resolve its recipe for review feedback flows, and dialog persistence can save scoped action settings.
